name: C Ionian
description: Bright, happy, uplifting — the natural major scale
commonUsage: Beethoven - Ode to Joy, John Denver - Take Me Home Country Roads

name: C Dorian
description: Minor with raised 6th — cool, jazzy, slightly hopeful
commonUsage: Santana - Oye Como Va, Miles Davis - So What, The Beatles - Eleanor Rigby

name: C Phrygian
description: Dark and Spanish-flavored, exotic and intense
commonUsage: Metallica - Wherever I May Roam, Flamenco music

name: C Lydian
description: Ethereal and dreamy, otherworldly brightness
commonUsage: John Williams - Superman Theme, Simpsons theme

name: C Mixolydian
description: Bluesy major — dominant, rock-friendly, funky
commonUsage: Sweet Home Chicago, Norwegian Wood - Beatles, Clocks - Coldplay

name: C Aeolian
description: Dark and emotional — the natural minor scale
commonUsage: Stairway to Heaven - Led Zeppelin, Losing My Religion - REM

name: C Locrian
description: Dissonant and unstable, rarely used — darkest mode
commonUsage: Björk - Army of Me, Some metal compositions
